page: Vultr vcg-a16-6c-64g-16vram is a GPU-accelerated virtual server designed for workloads requiring dedicated graphics acceleration and substantial system memory. It features 6 shared vCPUs powered by an Intel Broadwell processor running at 2.0 GHz, paired with 64.0 GB of system memory, which provides 10.67 GB of RAM per core. The server includes a bundled 350 GB NVMe SSD for local storage and one complimentary public IPv4 address.

The hardware configuration is highlighted by a single NVIDIA A16 GPU from the Ampere architecture, equipped with 16 GB of VRAM. Benchmark data indicates top-tier performance in LLM inference prompt processing, achieving 8771.1 tokens per second with a small 135M model and 591.21 tokens per second with a medium 7B model. Text generation speeds are also notable, reaching 296.92 tokens per second on the 135M model and 26.96 tokens per second on the 7B model.

In general compute and memory tasks, the server demonstrates average performance. The Intel Broadwell CPU registers a Passmark CPU score of 10693.5 and a stress-ng multi-core score of 10619.81. Memory bandwidth is measured at 586.08 GB/sec for cached small-block reads and 76.25 GB/sec for large-block reads. Database operations and web serving throughput are stable, with Redis SET operations processing at 10.78 million operations per second and static web serving throughput reaching 10.12 GB/sec. However, multi-threaded bzip3 decompression is a relative bottleneck, performing in the bottom 25% tier.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, this server offers a balanced entry point for GPU-accelerated tasks by combining a shared CPU architecture with a dedicated Ampere GPU and a high memory-to-vCPU ratio. This resource density makes it highly suitable for small-to-medium LLM inference, video transcoding, and memory-intensive graphics workloads, though the shared CPU allocation may introduce performance variability under sustained, non-GPU compute-heavy tasks.
description: Vultr vcg-a16-6c-64g-16vram is a GPU-accelerated virtual server configured with 6 shared Intel Broadwell vCPUs running at 2.0 GHz, 64.0 GB of system memory, and a 350 GB NVMe SSD. The system is equipped with a single NVIDIA A16 Ampere GPU featuring 16 GB of VRAM. Benchmark results show top-tier performance in LLM inference prompt processing, reaching 8771.1 tokens per second for a 135M model and 591.21 tokens per second for a 7B model. General compute and memory benchmarks, including a Passmark CPU score of 10693.5 and a large-block memory read bandwidth of 76.25 GB/sec, place the server in the average tier. This hardware profile is highly efficient for small-to-medium language model inference, media processing, and memory-intensive workloads that benefit from dedicated GPU acceleration without requiring dedicated high-frequency CPU cores.
og_description: Vultr vcg-a16-6c-64g-16vram is a GPU-accelerated server featuring 6 Intel vCPUs, 64 GB RAM, a 350 GB NVMe SSD, and an NVIDIA A16 GPU with 16 GB VRAM, optimized for small-to-medium LLM inference workloads.
meta_description: Vultr vcg-a16-6c-64g-16vram is a GPU server with 6 Intel vCPUs, 64 GB RAM, and an NVIDIA A16 GPU, delivering top-tier prompt processing performance.
tagline: A GPU-accelerated virtual server combining an NVIDIA Ampere GPU with high memory density for efficient small-to-medium model inference.
bullet_points: NVIDIA A16 Ampere GPU with 16 GB VRAM for hardware acceleration
6 shared Intel Broadwell vCPUs and 64.0 GB of system memory
350 GB NVMe SSD local storage and 1 public IPv4 address
Top-tier LLM prompt processing speeds for 135M and 7B models
Average general-purpose CPU and memory bandwidth performance
Best suited for small-to-medium LLM inference and media transcoding
